tagline: US withdrawal from global health and armed conflicts driven by demand for rare earth metals are key drivers behind an emerging Ebola disaster.

hero.title: The Smartphone Minerals War That Destroyed Congo's Health System — Just in Time for Ebola
hero.author: Celine Gounder, MD
hero.sourceName: What I can and Can't Say on TV
hero.excerpt: Rwanda’s mineral extraction, America’s aid cuts, and the most money-driven conflict one epidemiologist has ever seen.
hero.brief:
•	USAID 2025 freeze plus armed conflict driven by demand for rare earth metals hits East Congo in the same week
•	Resulting destruction of health system caused 300,000 excess deaths over the past year
•	The population could no longer rely on the clinics.
•	Then Ebola hit

selected.title: American contracts Ebola in DRC, headed to Germany with other exposed Americans. Expert analysis.
selected.author: Jeremy Faust, MD
selected.excerpt: Dr. Boghuma Titanji and I analyze the Trump administration’s new travel restrictions, the decision to send exposed Americans to Germany, and what the outbreak reveals about US readiness.
selected.brief:
•	Trump blocked entry of infected American doctor and exposed family
•	US bans non-US passport holders from DRC, Uganda, South Sudan. Against WHO guidance, amplifies stigma
•	US withdrawal means WHO can mobilize only $500K from its emergency fund

selected.title: Primary Series: Happy First Ebola Outbreak Situation Report Day!
selected.author: Angela Rasmussen, PhD
selected.excerpt: Each day brings fresh horrors, so we will keep you up to date on them
selected.brief:
•	>500 cases, 130+ deaths 
•	Cases now in Kampala, Goma, Bunia, Butembe, and possibly South Sudan
•	US withdrew from WHO and eliminated USAID. Now offer $10M. Too little too late
•	Risk of spread outside the outbreak zone is low. Risk to global health infrastructure is high

selected.title: The Burden of Caution
selected.author: Bruce Lanphear, MD
selected.excerpt: When scientific humility becomes public confusion
selected.brief:
•	The cautious language of epidemiology serves industries that profit from delay.
•	The tobacco playbook: weaponize scientific caution against science itself. Asbestos, lead, climate followed the same arc.
•	Humility is not evasion. Sometimes the honest word is "causes."

recent.title: Disease, Borders, and Blame: A Seven-Century Pattern
recent.author: Celine Gounder, MD
recent.sourceName: What I can and Can't Say on TV
recent.excerpt: From the Black Death to Ebola, disease control has been used to sort people by race and nationality. The pattern is centuries old, and it is happening again.

recent.title: Misinformation Masterclass
recent.author: Bob Morris, MD, PhD
recent.excerpt: A Wall Street Journal editor packs a pandemic’s worth of confusion into just 102 words

recent.title: An American doctor with Ebola was evacuated to Berlin. The U.S. has biocontainment units.
recent.author: Celine Gounder, MD
recent.sourceName: What I can and Can't Say on TV
recent.excerpt: What happened on Tuesday: the medevac, the 50 clinics, and what the State Department said about USAID.

recent.title: Breaking News: MV Hondius passenger at National Quarantine Unit intends to challenge a quarantine order she received on Monday.
recent.author: Jeremy Faust, MD
recent.excerpt: Dr. Jay Bhattacharya signed the order, requiring her to stay put, after she and others on board were exposed to Andes hantavirus.

recent.title: CDC Used Title 42 to Ban Travelers Over Ebola. Its Own Order Explains Why That Won’t Work.
recent.author: Celine Gounder, MD
recent.sourceName: What I can and Can't Say on TV
recent.excerpt: Title 42 sat unused for decades, then became an immigration tool during COVID. Now the CDC has invoked it for Ebola, a disease the order itself says can’t be transmitted by asymptomatic cases.

scientists.name: What I can and Can't Say on TV
scientists.author: Celine Gounder, MD
